summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #629 where MCP OAuth dynamic client registration fails with `invalid_redirect_uri` because Moltis sends `https://localhost` instead of `http://localhost` as the redirect URI.
summary.outcome: Implemented a `normalize_loopback_redirect` helper in `crates/mcp/src/auth.rs` that rewrites `https://` to `http://` for loopback hosts, leveraging the existing HTTP→HTTPS 301 redirect in the TLS layer. All 87 tests pass and the fix is committed.

repo: Moltis has a peek-based HTTP→HTTPS redirect in `crates/tls/src/lib.rs` (lines 472-615): if the first byte of a connection isn't a TLS handshake (0x16), it parses the request line and Host header, sends a 301 to `https://<host>:<port>/<path+query>`, preserving path and query string.
The `/auth/callback` route is unauthenticated (confirmed in `auth_middleware.rs:222`), so no session/cookie is required to complete the OAuth flow after the HTTP→HTTPS bounce.

The UI sends `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback` as `redirectUri` for MCP OAuth flows, which becomes `https://localhost:<port>/auth/callback` on loopback deployments — the root cause of the bug.
RFC 8252 §8.3 only applies to loopback redirects; real TLS hostnames like `https://moltis.lan` are accepted by strict AS's like Attio without issue.
The worktree for this work was at `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/boulder-penalty/`.
Project uses `bd` (beads) for issue tracking and `just` as a task runner with targets like `format-check`, `lint`, and `test`.

code.finding: Peek-based HTTP→HTTPS redirect implementation. Detects non-TLS connections, parses Host header, and 301 redirects preserving full path+query. Port is also preserved via Host header.
`McpOAuthProvider::start_web_oauth_flow` — where `redirect_uri` is used for dynamic client registration (RFC 7591) and the authorization request. This is where the normalizer was inserted.
Dynamic client registration uses `redirect_uri` as the sole entry in `redirect_uris` — strict AS's (e.g. Attio) reject `https://` scheme for loopback here per RFC 8252 §8.3.
Gateway plumbs `redirectUri` from UI through `mcp.oauth.start` / `mcp.add` / `mcp.enable` / `reauth` RPC calls to the MCP manager.
Manual OAuth override from `moltis.toml` (`[mcp.servers.*.oauth]`) is plumbed here via `build_auth_provider` — may not actually reach SSE remotes (secondary bug, unverified).
Builds OAuth redirect URI from `window.location.origin`, producing `https://localhost:<port>/auth/callback` on loopback deployments.
`CallbackServer::wait_for_code` exists for in-process loopback callback handling, with a 60s timeout. Not needed for this fix but relevant to alternative approaches considered.

workflow: The initial issue number was wrong (#630 vs #629) — always verify issue numbers before diving into research.
The simplest fix leveraged existing infrastructure (HTTP→HTTPS redirect) rather than adding new components (loopback callback server), reducing the change to ~20 lines of production code.

The design went through three iterations: (1) loopback callback server, (2) branch on origin strategy, (3) simple scheme rewrite leveraging existing TLS redirect — each triggered by a user question revealing a constraint.
Used `cargo nextest run -p moltis-mcp` for targeted crate testing rather than full workspace tests.
Format check required a fix before the commit — always run `just format-check` before committing in this repo.

friction: Initial issue number mismatch (#630 vs #629) required clarification before work could begin.
The design required three full iterations due to incrementally revealed constraints (non-loopback deployments like `moltis.lan`, then the existing HTTP→HTTPS redirect discovery).
Format check failed after initial implementation and required a fixup edit before the commit.
open_items: Secondary bug: `[mcp.servers.<name>.oauth]` manual override in `moltis.toml` may be silently ignored for SSE remote servers — needs a separate investigation and `bd` issue.

Provider OAuth (`provider-oauth.js`) has the same `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback` pattern and the same potential loopback-scheme bug — apply `normalize_loopback_redirect` if/when an AS rejects it there too.
Consider whether `CallbackServer::wait_for_code` 60s timeout should be configurable via a const or config option.
E2E test coverage for MCP OAuth with loopback scenario was noted as 'optional but recommended' and not implemented in this session.
IPv6-only host support: if `127.0.0.1` won't bind, consider falling back to `::1` — not handled in current fix.
